prose:
**Fukui Reactivity Analysis of Benzaldehyde** — DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz`, used as-is (single-point, not re-optimized). Charge scheme: Mulliken populations. SCF converged for neutral, cation (+1, doublet), and anion (−1, doublet) states; charge conservation checks passed (∑f⁺ = 1.0000, ∑f⁻ = 1.0000). **Most electrophilic site:** atom 3 (aromatic C ortho to CHO), f⁺ = 0.39939 (dual descriptor = 0.36264). **Most nucleophilic site:** atom 1 (carbonyl O), f⁻ = 0.23840. Fukui indices measure reactivity preferences: f⁺ indicates electrophilic susceptibility, f⁻ nucleophilic, f⁰ their average, and the dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) ranks electrophilic vs. nucleophilic character. The carbonyl oxygen dominates nucleophilicity, while ortho aromatic carbons (atoms 3, 8) show the strongest electrophilicity, consistent with typical benzaldehyde reactivity. Result produced by chemkit fukui skill using PySCF backend. Integrity status: trustworthy (all checks passed).</prose>
